data.title: Coinbase Stock Shoots Up 15% even as Analysts Slash COIN Price Forecasts after Q4 Miss
data.description:
Coinbase stock (COIN) is shaking off a rough earnings report. Even though the company reported its first quarterly loss in two years, the share price surged 15.8% during the first hour of trading. This sharp climb happened even as a quiet crypto market forced the exchange to watch its trading volumes slide and its token holdings lose value. While the financials look messy on paper, investors seem to care more about the company's plan to return billions of dollars in cash to shareholders.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts Lower the Ceiling, but Remain Bullish on COIN Stock

Major Wall Street banks were quick to update their math after the report came out Thursday evening. JPMorgan's Kenneth Worthington kept its "overweight" rating but lowered its price target to $252 from $290, noting that users are shifting toward lower-fee trading tools. Canaccord's Joseph Vafi made an even bigger cut, dropping its target from $400 down to $300. These experts are watching the "take rate," the slice of each trade Coinbase keeps as profit, which is getting thinner as more people sign up for the Coinbase One subscription instead of paying per trade.

Coinbase Experienced a Massive Loss

The actual numbers for the end of 2025 were undeniably tough. Coinbase swung to a net loss of $666.7 million, a total reversal from the $1.3 billion profit it saw during the crypto boom a year earlier. Total revenue fell about 20% to $1.78 billion, coming in lower than what Wall Street expected. The company also took a $718 million hit just because the value of the Bitcoin and Ethereum it holds in its own vault dropped during the market slump.

Why COIN Stock Is Surging Anyway

The reason for today's double-digit jump comes down to a few key moves by the company:

  • Massive Buybacks: Coinbase has already spent $1.7 billion buying back its own stock recently to help keep the price steady.
  • Dry Powder for Growth: The Board just approved an additional $2 billion for even more buybacks, signaling they think the stock is currently a steal.
  • New Revenue Streams: The company is successfully moving into prediction markets and stock trading, which helps them make money even when Bitcoin isn't doing much.
  • Resilient Buyers: CFO Alesia Haas mentioned that while prices are volatile, their customers have actually been net buyers during this latest price drop.

Key Takeaway

Essentially, the market is choosing to ignore the ugly numbers from last year. Yes, the company lost a lot of money because Bitcoin fell, but investors are focused on the fact that Coinbase is sitting on a massive pile of cash, over $11 billion. The company is showing a huge amount of confidence through its decision to use that money to buy back its own shares. If they can keep expanding into things like stocks and prediction markets, they might finally stop being a stock that only goes up and down with the price of Bitcoin.

Is Coinbase a Good Stock to Buy?

Coinbase stock (COIN) has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 23 Wall Street analysts. This rating is based on 14 Buy, seven Hold, and two Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average 12-month COIN price target of $275 implies 69.5% upside from current levels. These ratings could continue to change over the next few days.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: AC Earnings: Air Canada Stock Rises as Carrier Returns to Profitability
data.description:
Air Canada's (TSE:AC) stock is up 5% after the company returned to profitability in the fourth quarter of 2025.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Montreal-based airline, which is Canada's flagship carrier, reported C$296 million in net income during Q4 2025, up from a loss of C$644 million a year earlier. Air Canada announced earnings per share of C$0.47, which beat the consensus estimate among analysts of C$0.20.

Revenue in the final three months of last year amounted to C$5.8 billion, up 7% from C$5.4 billion a year ago. Analysts had expected Air Canada's revenue for Q4 2025 to be in a range of C$5.51 billion to C$5.56 billion. The company has struggled since the COVID-19 pandemic struck in 2020.

Aircraft Acquisition

Air Canada CEO Michael Rousseau praised the results, saying that the carrier managed a return to profitability despite shifting travel demands among consumers and amid macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainty.

Along with its latest financial results, Air Canada announced the purchase of eight new Airbus (EADSY) A350-1000 wide-body aircraft, with rights to purchase an additional eight planes at a future date. In recent days, the airline suspended flights to Cuba due to a fuel shortage in the Caribbean nation.

Is AC Stock a Buy?

Air Canada's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among eight Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on five Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average AC price target of C$23.32 implies 15.67% upside from current levels. These ratings could change after the company's financial results.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Trump Plans to Scale Back Steel, Aluminum Tariffs
data.description:
Last June, President Trump imposed a 50% tariff on steel and aluminum imports. However, aluminum prices have soared by 24% since then, while steel has risen about 4%, heightening an affordability crisis faced by many Americans.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Now, the Trump administration is planning to dial back these tariffs, starting with a duty exemption on some steel and aluminum products, according to the Financial Times. In addition, the administration will conduct narrower national security investigations into specific products that could face tariffs, a move that contrasts with its previous sweeping approach.

Americans Pay for 90% of Trump's Tariffs, Says New York Fed

Sources told the FT that the Commerce Department believes current tariffs have affected consumers, raising the prices of products including drink cans and pie tins.

The news comes amid a study from the New York Fed, which found that Americans are paying for 90% of the increased costs from tariffs. The average tariff rate on U.S. imports has risen to 13% from 2.6%, although exporters aren't absorbing those costs. Instead, they have kept prices steady, while importers have passed on higher costs to consumers.

data.title: Is the XRP Crash Over? This Rare On-Chain Signal Is Flashing a Buy for February 2026
data.description:
XRP's price (XRP-USD) is showing signs of strength this Friday as it fights to stay above its daily low of $1.35. After a rough week where the token failed to keep its footing above $1.40, traders are searching for any hint of a turnaround. While the general crypto market feels a bit shaky, specific data points suggest that XRP might be bottoming out. This means the price could be reaching the lowest point of its recent slide before starting a slow climb back up.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On-chain Signal Flashes a "Buy"

One of the most interesting clues comes from a metric called the MVRV-Z Score. This tool helps investors figure out if a coin is cheap or expensive compared to its fair value. Right now, this score has been in the negative zone for nearly two weeks. In the past, whenever this happened, it was usually followed by a massive price jump. For example, back in July 2024, a similar negative score was the starting point for XRP to skyrocket from $0.45 all the way to $2.91 just a few months later.

Exchanges Get Flooded with XRP Tokens

However, it's not all good news. Data shows that the amount of XRP sitting on exchanges has actually been increasing lately, hitting about 12.99 billion tokens. This is usually a sign of caution. When people move their coins onto exchanges, it often means they are getting ready to sell. This extra supply can act like a heavy weight, making it harder for the price to move up even if more people start buying.

Key XRP Price Levels to Watch

For XRP to really start a recovery, it needs to break back above the $1.40 level and stay there. If it can do that, it might clear the path to test higher prices again. But if the selling continues and the price drops below $1.30, the next safety net isn't until $1.25 or even the psychological floor of $1.00.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.3785.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Why Are All 'Magnificent Seven' Stocks Down Today?
data.description:
All "Mag 7" stocks — Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), Nvidia (NVDA), Meta Platforms (META), and Tesla (TSLA) — were down again in early Friday trading after the group closed lower Thursday amid rising caution around big tech and AI spending.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Apple drew the most attention after reports said its planned AI upgrade to Siri may be delayed. The stock fell about 5%, its worst day since April 2025. Because Apple is one of the largest weights in major indexes, its drop pulled sentiment down across the group. Investors are also watching whether new AI features can drive the next iPhone upgrade cycle, making any delay more sensitive for the stock.

There was broader evidence of caution as well. UBS recently downgraded the U.S. technology sector to Neutral, warning that heavy AI spending and uncertainty around software demand could pressure near-term returns.

Investors are also watching rising spending plans. Mark Hawtin of Liontrust Asset Management (LIO) recently said the rapid increase in capital expenditure across the "Magnificent Seven" could become a risk, as companies invest heavily in AI while current revenue from the technology remains limited. He cited Amazon as an example, noting much of its expected cash flow this year could be absorbed by rising capital spending.

What the Group Decline Signals

When these companies fall together, it usually means investors are reducing risk in the market's most crowded trade. The "Magnificent Seven" have powered a large share of market gains over the past two years, so weakness in the group can weigh on the wider indexes.

The move does not point to weak earnings. Instead, it reflects concern about future growth — specifically how quickly artificial intelligence will translate into profits for the biggest technology companies.

Which Magnificent 7 Stock Is the Better Buy? 

Turning to Wall Street, out of the Magnificent 7 stocks mentioned above, analysts think that Microsoft stock has the most room to run. In fact, Microsoft's average price target of $593.38 per share implies 47.7% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Apple Stock Fell 5% in Its Worst Day Since April — The Real Reason Wasn't Just Siri
data.description:
Apple, the iPhone maker and the world's most valuable public company, fell about 5% Thursday in its worst session since April 2025. The drop erased the stock's gains for the year and left shares down roughly 4% in 2026. However, the shares were trading slightly higher in pre-market Friday, suggesting investors may be reassessing the sell-off.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The decline followed a Bloomberg report that Apple's planned AI upgrade to Siri may be delayed. The rollout has reportedly been pushed to May or later instead of arriving in the coming weeks. However, Apple said it still expects to release the features in 2026.

But the reaction on Wall Street suggested the concern was larger than a simple product delay. Investors have been waiting to see Apple's AI plan. While many tech companies have already launched new AI tools, Apple has moved more slowly. Because of that, even a short delay raised worries that the company could fall behind rivals in adding new features to its devices.

Regulatory Pressure Added to Worries

On the same day, Apple also drew regulatory attention. It was accused of "censoring conservatives" in its news feed. Federal Trade Commission Chair Andrew Ferguson sent CEO Tim Cook a letter asking the company to review how its Apple News app selects and presents content after reports of potential bias.

The development did not directly affect sales, but it added another uncertainty for investors already reacting to the Siri news.

Why the Market Reacted So Strongly

The sell-off also came as sentiment toward large technology companies has recently weakened. Many investors now question whether heavy AI spending will pay off soon. Software stocks have already fallen in recent days, and analysts recently cut their view on the U.S. tech sector.

Apple actually reported strong earnings last month. But markets care more about future growth than past results. For Apple, that growth depends on whether AI features can drive new iPhone upgrades.

So Thursday's fall was not just about a delayed feature. The market was reacting to a bigger worry, that Apple's next growth driver, AI, may take longer than investors expected.

Is AAPL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Apple has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 27 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 17 Buy, nine Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average AAPL price target of $306.89 implies 17.25% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
